items.title: 'Straight to landfill': Compostable packaging but nowhere to compost it

children.content: The ACT mandates reusable, recyclable and compostable packaging at all public events, but nearly all of it ends up in landfill because there is no facility to compost it.

gallery.altText: A meal served on a compostable paper plate with wooden cutlery.

thumbnail.altText: A meal served on a compostable paper plate with wooden cutlery.

items.title: A man fled a 'bounty' in Canberra. Months later, he was dead in Brisbane

children.content: Neil Tracey's death was ruled an overdose in what investigators would call an open and shut case. His family are questioning if police knew about the bounty that led him to flee his hometown.

image.altText: Graphic of a man's smiling face with newspapers articles surrounding it.

items.title: Court hears of 'tragic mistake' that caused fatal Canberra crash

children.content: The daughters of a motorcyclist killed in Canberra's north tried to reach him for their nightly phone call as he lay dying on the road, an ACT court has heard.

image.altText: A man with a beard smiles, wearing a hat that says Harley Davidson, sitting inside an eatery.

items.title: Heated Rivalry, Off Campus driving ice hockey boom in Canberra

children.content: Women now outnumber men as attendees to Canberra Brave ice hockey games — thanks in part to a surge in popularity for the sport inspired by the recent shows Heated Rivalry and Off Campus.

gallery.altText: Two men in tuxedos with their foreheads touching.
Ella Bright and Belmont Cameli intimately close in a lecture theatre in a scene from Off Campus.
People play ice hockey on a rink in a darkened stadium.

thumbnail.altText: Two men in tuxedos with their foreheads touching.
Ella Bright and Belmont Cameli intimately close in a lecture theatre in a scene from Off Campus.
People play ice hockey on a rink in a darkened stadium.

items.title: Budget blowout in Queanbeyan with 100yo pipe found in unexpected spot

children.content: A century-old stormwater drain nearly 2 metres thick is uncovered beneath Queanbeyan's town centre, forcing the council to extend major roadworks and source extra funding.

image.altText: An old pip dug up in the ground.

items.title: Parton says Canberrans are 'more vulnerable' after budget's release

children.content: The Canberra Liberals and the ACT Greens issue their responses to the ACT budget, criticising the government's fiscal plan, principally around cost of living, transport and overall management of the territory's finances.

image.altText: Mark Parton and Leanne Castley

items.title: Canberra's only water park will go up for sale. Here's why that's good news

children.content: The ACT government says the owners are not Canberra-based and had thought they were investing in something very different.

gallery.altText: Three tower waterslides, all in a significant state of disrepair, surrounded by gum trees.

thumbnail.altText: Three tower waterslides, all in a significant state of disrepair, surrounded by gum trees.

items.title: Satellite images reveal missing Antarctic ice the 'size of France'

children.content: Scientists say a chunk of winter sea ice the size of France off the Antarctic coast has failed to form this year.

gallery.altText: An aerial view of white ice breaking up on a black sea

thumbnail.altText: An aerial view of white ice breaking up on a black sea

avatar.alt: A woman smiling at the camera.

items.title: Knicks end 53-year drought to win NBA championship

children.content: New York Knicks come from behind to defeat San Antonio Spurs 94-90 in Game 5 of the NBA Finals.

image.altText: The New York Knicks celebrate with the Larry O'Brien Championship Trophy.

items.title: Saints, GWS going goal for goal in Docklands thriller

children.content: The Tigers host Brisbane in Hobart, before the Saints and Giants clash at Docklands to bring round 14 of the AFL to a close. Follow live.

image.altText: An AFL player in orange takes a mark above a player in black

items.title: African scam victims left homeless in Cambodia amid crackdown

children.content: Hundreds of trafficking survivors from Africa are left stranded in Cambodia amid a widespread crackdown on the country's scam compounds.

image.altText: Two sets of handcuffs.

items.title: Police officer in custody after Perth girl shot dead overseas

children.content: A nine-year-old Perth girl has been fatally shot by police in Pakistan after officers mistook her family's car for that of suspected criminals.

image.altText: A silhouette of two Pakistani police officers holding rifles at a barrier or gate of some sort.

items.title: Meet the Brisbane couple who have donated more than $100 million to charity

children.content: Quentin and Kylie Birt say they were inspired to donate at the Big Freeze event after reading Neale Daniher's book.

gallery.altText: A woman and man in hiking gear surrounded by trees.

thumbnail.altText: A woman and man in hiking gear surrounded by trees.

avatar.alt: A young woman with blonde hair, smiling at the camera.

items.title: Butler open to NDIS changes but convinced it's heading in 'right direction'

children.content: Health Minister Mark Butler is open to adjusting the proposed National Disability Insurance Scheme changes, but insists the government is on the right track.

image.altText: Mark Butler standing in a corridor in a suit

items.title: Thousands rally in Belfast to condemn anti-immigrant rioting

children.content: The anti-racism rally came after nights of fiery violence in parts of Northern Ireland following the arrest of a 30-year-old man from Sudan on charges of attempted murder in a stabbing that left a man partly blind.

image.altText: A large crowd holds up anti-racism signs in the middle of a city.

items.title: Love story: When these childhood friends reunited, the spark was undeniable

children.content: It was a case of puppy love when Cambell first set eyes on Tiana at a Gold Coast housing complex when they were both nine years old. They lost touch when they entered their teens but reunited as adults and have been inseparable since.

gallery.altText: Tiana Storm and Cambell Priest smile for the camera in a posed portrait photograph.

thumbnail.altText: Tiana Storm and Cambell Priest smile for the camera in a posed portrait photograph.

items.title: The feminist and the bra fitter: Inside the world of Hannah Ferguson and her mum

children.content: Emerging media star Hannah Ferguson and her mum, professional bra fitter Phillipa Mitchell, have found themselves on a similar path of empowering women, but just don't expect them to see it that way.

image.altText: An older mother and her late twenties daughter look deeply at each other, chatting over cups of tea.

items.title: Tinariwen deliver songs of hope from a homeland in crisis

children.content: Legendary African band Tinariwen say there’s very little hope for their country. It just makes their dedication to music more important.

image.altText: Tinariwen stand beside a Toyota pickup truck in a rocky desert landscape with a mountain in the background

items.title: Some think Britain's 'broken', and they are looking to Australia for inspiration

children.content: Right-wing populism is rising in the UK where, like Australia, the political landscape is looking very different lately. It has prompted some people to ask if these surging parties can deliver on their promises.

image.altText: A man in a suit smiling and gesturing at the camera, while a group of people behind him hold signs in support of Reform UK.

items.title: Brisbane to get $5.6m boost to health and wellness programs

children.content: The Move Well Brisbane campaign will double the number of providers and activities in Brisbane's Active and Healthy program and include nutritional and cooking classes.

image.altText: Silhouettes of people jogging along the river in the morning.

items.title: The multi-million-dollar tourist hotspot where locals can't afford to live

children.content: A tourist hotspot with skyrocketing rents, low rental vacancies and a dearth of public housing, has found a way to help find homes for those who can't afford to live in their own region.

image.altText: A young woman with flowing hair stands amid the leaves and flowering gumnuts of a eucalypt tree

items.title: 'Who will be accountable for this?': Families grieve sailors killed in Strait of Hormuz

children.content: Families of Indian sailors who died in a US military attack in the Strait of Hormuz want to know who is accountable for attacks on commercial vessels.

image.altText: Three Indian sailors have died in a US military operation to halt a tanker off Oman

items.title: King Charles celebrates official birthday with Trooping the Colour

children.content: Members of the royal family attend the king's annual birthday parade in London.

items.title: Hundreds of regional firefighters rally against levy in Bendigo

children.content: Hundreds of regional Victorian firefighters turn out for a rally in Bendigo, calling on the state government to scrap a controversial levy after saying they have been "left behind".

image.altText: A group of people wearing yellow CFA jackets.

items.title: 'We have all got Tino's back': Maroons rally around mourning star

children.content: Tino Fa'asuamaleaui indicates he plans to play in Origin II after the death of his father this week.

image.altText: GettyImages-2278423795

items.title: Here's what you need to know about the KPMG scandal

children.content: Accounting firm KPMG is engulfed in a scandal that has seen several senior leaders quit and ASIC launch an investigation into the firm's business dealings.

image.altText: A large, grey building with a blue and white sign at the top reading KPMG.

items.title: Former potato farm given new life as bird sanctuary

children.content: Once drained for farm irrigation, this former potato farm now attracts birds from as far away as Siberia.

image.altText: a brown bird in flight

items.title: Why older Australians with MND are considering early euthanasia

children.content: The death of AFL legend Neale Daniher has shone a light on the grim choices faced by older Australians fighting motor neurone disease.

items.title: Woman ditches custom wedding gown for $90 op shop dream dress

children.content: Kaila Scally-Chadwick abandoned her previously purchased made-to-order gown when she found her dream dress in a second-hand shop just months out from the big day.

image.altText: Baby wearing white standing holding the train of a lace wedding dress and looking at the camera with mum crouched beside

items.title: From wine to water, Adelaide Hills charity brings hope to kids in Kenya

children.content: From the Adelaide Hills to refugee camps in Kenya, how one young Aussie has made it his mission to help.

image.altText: A group of young boys smile and pose for a photo in rural Kenya.

items.title: Soccer tournament unites refugees through universal language of sport

children.content: A soccer tournament in Perth's north helps participants overcome barriers, while inspiring more women to pursue the game, as part of Refugee Week.

image.altText: A woman wears a red jumper and holds a soccer ball out to the camera.

items.title: A cafe run by students, for students aims to tackle food insecurity

children.content: It might look like a regular cafe, but a new student-run business in Hobart's CBD has a different goal than turning a profit — a mission to feed their struggling peers.

image.altText: A woman smiles for a photo in a mini grocery store

items.title: Why young people say the social media ban is not working six months on

children.content: Research suggests many under-16s are still active on social media platforms. BTN High explores why and tests the accuracy of popular technology used to estimate age.

items.title: Police charge Tennant Creek teenager with 21-year-old's stabbing murder

children.content: Northern Territory Police charge a 17-year-old boy with the murder of a 21-year-old man who he allegedly stabbed to death in Tennant Creek last week.
